title: centaurea leucophaea
intro: Centaurea leucophaea, also known as the white knapweed, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia, known for its white or pinkish flowers and fibrous roots. The medicinal properties of Centaurea leucophaea lie in its roots, which contain a complex mixture of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the roots of Centaurea leucophaea are used to make infusions, tinctures, and teas that are believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function.
